title: Telangana Bagara Rice

ingredients.name: basmati rice, rinsed and soaked 20 minutes

subs.name: sona masoori rice; reduce water to 2 cups as it absorbs less
oil only; the flavour is less rich but still excellent

steps.text: Heat ghee and oil together in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high flame. Add cumin seeds, bay leaves, cinnamon, cloves, cardamom and star anise; fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the sliced onion and fry, stirring often, until it turns a deep golden-brown, about 10 minutes. This caramelised onion is what gives bagara rice its characteristic depth.
Add green chillies, curry leaves and mint; stir for 30 seconds. Add ginger-garlic paste and fry until the raw smell disappears, about 1 minute.
Drain the soaked rice and add it to the pot. Stir gently to coat every grain in the ghee and spices. Toast for 2 minutes — the rice will turn opaque.
Add 2.5 cups hot water and salt. Stir once, increase heat to high and bring to a boil. Cover tightly, reduce heat to the lowest setting and cook for 12 minutes.
Remove from heat and rest, undisturbed with the lid on, for 8 minutes. Fluff gently with a fork. Serve with a gravy curry, raita or pappu (dal).

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
